name: Replicate API
what_it_does: Cloud platform for running open-source machine learning models via API — including image generation, LLMs, audio, and video — with pay-per-second GPU billing and official Python/Node clients.
use_cases: Running Stable Diffusion and other image generation models from agents
Inference with open-source LLMs (Llama, Mistral, etc.) via simple API
Video generation and processing via cloud GPU models
Audio transcription and generation using hosted models
Rapid prototyping with diverse ML models without GPU infrastructure
not_for: Production serving of models at high throughput (latency is variable)
Fine-tuning models at scale (better done with dedicated ML platforms)
Teams needing data privacy guarantees (inputs are sent to Replicate)
Sub-100ms inference requirements (cold start latency applies)
best_when: An agent needs access to diverse open-source ML models for image, audio, or text processing without managing GPU infrastructure.
avoid_when: You need guaranteed low latency, model privacy, or high-throughput production inference.

auth.notes: Simple API token authentication. Token from Replicate account settings. Full account access per token — no scopes or permissions granularity. Very straightforward for agents.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No ongoing free tier; new accounts may receive trial credits.
pricing.paid_tiers: Pay per second of GPU compute. Image generation ~$0.0023/run; LLMs priced per token. Varies significantly by model.
pricing.notes: No free tier after trial credits are exhausted. Credit card required to continue. Pay per model run based on GPU seconds. Cost-effective for sporadic usage.

performance.rate_limits: Depends on plan; typically 10-50 concurrent predictions

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses include detail field with human-readable messages. Model-specific errors are surfaced clearly. Timeout errors and cold start states are well-documented. Prediction status polling is straightforward.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Each prediction creates a new run with a unique ID. No idempotency keys. Agents should track prediction IDs to avoid duplicate runs on retry.
known_agent_gotchas: Predictions are asynchronous by default — agents must poll for completion or configure webhooks
Cold start latency can add 10-60 seconds for infrequently used models
Model versions can be deprecated — agents must pin to specific version IDs or use latest tag carefully
Input schema varies significantly per model — agents must inspect model schema before calling
Large file outputs (images, video) are URLs that expire after 1 hour — download promptly
Concurrent prediction limits apply — agents running many parallel predictions may be throttled
Credit card required — agent pipelines will silently fail if billing lapses

score_rationale: Replicate offers an excellent breadth of ML models with a clean REST API that is very agent-accessible. fq_score reflects the good API design, SDK coverage across many languages, and webhook support. af_score is dragged down by mandatory credit card (blocking automated provisioning), no idempotency, expiring output URLs that require prompt handling, and highly variable latency from cold starts. Well-suited for agents doing creative/generative work but not for latency-sensitive workflows.
